description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four dark metal hoops. The wood has a natural, light tan finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The metal hoops are evenly spaced, providing structural reinforcement and a contrasting dark brown color. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with no visible openings or spouts. There are no handles, markings, or decorative elements present on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban, industrial, or outdoor settings.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with four dark metal hoops and a natural tan wood finish, featuring a bulging cylindrical shape and flat top and bottom surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, four metal hoops, and the flat wooden top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Side view with a clear look at the wood grain and the continuous banding of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Opposite angle showing the same features, with subtle differences in wood texture and lighting.
